notes: UAE, Qatar, Bahrain, Kuwait FIRs severely disrupted. DXB <20% capacity; 12,000+ flights cancelled. Only Oman Muscat FIR remains open in Gulf. Multiple NOTAMs active across region.

Multiple Iran land borders closed/restricted. Saudi-Yemen crossings closed (conflict). Saudi-Iraq restricted (militia activity). UAE-Saudi route active but NOT RECOMMENDED due to Eastern Province strikes.

IRGC declared Strait 'closed' Mar 2. Near-zero commercial maritime traffic. Indiscriminate area denial ops in Gulf of Oman. Tanker struck off Musandam (4 injured). 20% of global oil supply blocked. War-risk insurance expires Mar 5 — commercial shipping impossible after.

Bloomberg (Mar 2): UAE projected to exhaust interceptors within ~1 week at current rate. UAE officially denies. US reportedly stonewalling resupply requests. PAC-3 ~$4M/interceptor; THAAD ~$12M/interceptor. Critical window: Mar 7-10. See BM Tracker section for daily stats.

Hezbollah opened second front vs Israel Mar 2; Israel ground invasion of Lebanon authorized. Houthis signaled Red Sea attack resumption (not yet executed). Iraqi Shia militias active; 6 US service members KIA in Kuwait. Multi-front theater expanding.

IAEA Board session ongoing through Mar 7. Active conflict complicates monitoring access. Barakah reactor (UAE) flagged as low-probability but catastrophic risk. Iranian nuclear facilities potentially in US/Israeli strike queue.
No reported civil unrest. Government maintaining order. Foreign nationals (20,000+ stranded) showing signs of panic-buying and evacuation pressure. Monitor for labor/expat community breakdown.
No reported civil unrest. Government maintaining information control. Eastern Province population (near energy targets) under stress. No protest activity reported.
Stable. Muscat unaffected by strikes. Population calm. Serving as regional evacuation hub. No protest or civil disturbance activity.

Bahrain hosts US 5th Fleet (NSA Bahrain). Residential tower struck by drone Mar 1 (Era Views Towers, Manama — video confirmed). Shia population (majority) historically sympathetic to Iran. Monitor for unrest capitalizing on strikes.

AWS data center fire Mar 2 (cause under investigation). GPS spoofing/jamming active across Gulf region. Iranian cyber units historically target critical infrastructure during kinetic escalation. No confirmed major attribution yet.
